response: The three main psychedelic compounds found in Psilocybin genus mushrooms that are converted to psilocin by the body are psilocybin, psilocin, and baeocystin. Psilocybin is quickly converted by the body to psilocin, which has mind-altering effects similar to those of LSD, mescaline, and DMT. Psilocybin and psilocin are both naturally occurring compounds, while baeocystin is an analog of psilocybin and is also converted to psilocin.
